title: Level 231
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 231: "Vintage Habitation"}}__NOTOC__ {{Liminal Theme}} {{Foreground|File=Static for levlel 230.gif|Opacity=0.1}} {{Class Custom Image |class=0 |hexagon=0 |safety=Safe |security=Unstable |entity=Devoid of Entities |bg-image=Im-safe-in-here-they-cant-hurt-me-here.jpg |text-color=white}} ==⤳ Description== {{Blurimage|file=Im-safe-in-here-they-cant-hurt-me-here.jpg|caption=An iconic picture of Level 231.|width=350px|align=right}}'''Level 231''', addressed by many names such as "A Place Where I Call Home" or "Vintage Habitation", features an indefinite series of interconnected rooms and hallways reminiscent of those found in suburban basements from the early seventies era. A vast majority of the level comprises of yellowed walls accompanied with carpet, however, certain rooms resembling either kitchens or laundry areas are composed of pristine, plaster rooms. Illumination, although dim and infrequent, comes in the shape of various lamps. Nevertheless, small rectangular windows may be found near the ceilings of the aforementioned kitchen-like rooms, where a blue light seeps into them. Entropic hazards are rare to come across whilst traversing the halls of '''Level 231''', mainly consisting of sudden shifts in one´s current location and slight alterations in the environment. A staircase may appear longer than it really is, whilst certain rooms may repeat recursively. Structural repetition can be found alongside the unstable environments throughout Level 231, where exact replicas of a single room can be found everywhere throughout the level. Whilst phenomena such as these can manifest throughout the familiar expanse that the level poses, many of the instances are thought to be mere hallucinations within one´s mind. This is due to scouting missions involving more than one person, which revealed that some personnel viewed specific spaces differently than others, likely proving the aforementioned theory. {{Blurimage|file=Level273abovethebasement.jpeg|width=350px|hovertext=Nonsensical architecture|caption=Another picture taken of the level, featuring brown carpeting instead of a beige one.}} The only known method of entering the other floors on '''Level 231''' is through cramped stairways. They are nonlinear in shape, typically leading in multiple directions, and are seldom cut midway by nearby corridors. Furthermore, they are adorned with a wooden fence and, sporadically, a semi-circular brown carpet in front. While broadly consistent in design, these floors can vary in coloration and whether furniture is present. Their structural design may falter depending on how close one is to the highest floor. Reportedly, on said floor, carpet fluid droplets fall from the ceiling, and a faint humming can be heard throughout the level. The contents of the lowest floors are comprised of small rooms, no longer than 12 meters, all following a recursive pattern connected by stairs. Level 231’s temperature fluctuates depending on one's location. Some regions reach slightly frigid temperatures; others are said to be no higher than forty-five degrees Celsius, while the bulk remains at a stable twenty degrees Celsius. Additionally, multiple wanderers claim to have seen snow accumulating inside particular rooms on the level, but this has yet to be verified. There are documents of the color of the lights shifting upon a temperature change, where a frigid room will be illuminated in a dark blue hue, while other sections increasing in temperature appear in warmer colors. Causes of these temperature fluctuations include a wide array of cooling systems, which include functioning air conditioning machines, ceiling fans, and air vents. Various sounds can occasionally be heard from different directions throughout the level, such as knocking, wood creaking, faint whispering, and the rumbling of machinery. The cause of these noises remains unclear, but some say that they are the result of auditory hallucinations. There have been reports of these sounds originating from the aforementioned blue void or behind the walls, although what exactly causes these sounds to play remains a mystery. Sounds throughout the level are usually heard at a much higher pitch, approximately 25 decibels higher than their standard volume. Some areas of the level are almost entirely barren of anything in an average house, besides the occasional empty moving box. The key difference setting it apart is the maze-like architecture. Blacked-out windows, stairways that lead nowhere, and inconsistent lighting are all prevalent features of these areas. Colors in these maze-like sections are slightly distinct from the level´s other parts, comprising of a desaturated, light brown or tan accompanied by carpet and floor tiles. Whilst more challenging to traverse, areas that follow these maze designs are devoid of alterations caused by [[Phenomenon List|certain phenomena]], and are therefore spots to look for. ==⤳ Phenomena== A wide array of strange phenomena has appeared throughout the expanse of '''Level 231''', dating back to the original discovery of the level. Written accounts hold entries about furniture hanging throughout the walls and ceilings, as well as manifestations of [[Phenomenon 57|Achronism]] and [[Phenomenon 11]] throughout. {{Blurimage|file=Vanishingspaces273.jpeg|width=350px|hovertext=Unreality|caption=The only picture taken of this phenomenon.}} ===⤳ Vanishing Spaces=== Those venturing throughout '''Level 231''' have reported certain sections of the level shaking and eventually collapsing into an infinite blue sky. Whilst occurring very rarely, documentation of said phenomena is vast. It is known that numerous warnings are applied hours before the collapse of these areas, which mainly include earthquakes, cracks of moderate size on the walls, and an obstructive buzzing sound emanating beyond the walls and floors. The skies themselves expand indefinitely in all directions, lacking an end. This sky is thought to be the same blue void that seeps light into certain rooms; however, as clouds are not seen from the windows themselves, this is widely debated. Additionally, wanderers who have collapsed downwards into the sky remember vague, dreamlike memories of falling. Somehow, everyone who experienced this fall had found themselves in a different area of the level afterwards. Whilst not deadly, specific side effects remain on wanderers after falling from an area affected by Vanishing Spaces, including frequent hypnic jerks and a slight attraction to levels containing a cloudy sky. ===⤳ Bliss=== Numerous rooms throughout '''Level 231''' hold manifestations of a phenomenon labeled as "bliss". This phenomenon occurs upon prolonged exposure to one of said rooms, in which a wanderer will start entering stages of bliss and drowsiness. If not erased upon exiting the anomalous room, these effects may worsen. While not generally harmful, deep states of drowsiness may weaken one, posing as an easier target for lurking entities. Additionally, such effects often lead to hallucinations. Anomalous rooms are characterized by a certain saturation, in which specific colors may appear more intense. Said saturation comes alongside alterations in a wanderer´s visual function, where a grainy effect can be noted in the rooms, similar to malfunctioning televisions. In addition, the rooms may appear to visually distort themselves, making it hard to exit them. One is advised never to settle a temporary basecamp in such rooms, as initial effects appear in mere minutes. {{Blurimage|file=Echoes liminal 2.png|align=center|width=600px|hovertext=Nostalgia|caption=One of the barren areas of Level 231.}}
Fragments of My Past

A long time ago, probably just a few days after my sixth birthday, my father bought a small house in the suburbs of Richmond. Surprisingly, I was looking forward to this new house, as I hadn’t really made any friends at school. To me, moving away felt like a fresh beginning — one that I would take advantage of. I can say I definitely succeeded, as peers from my new school welcomed me with open arms. It didn’t take long for me to make friends and invite them to my house, as every kid would do. The house itself was not very big, but enough for a family of three. That same house then became the background for most of my treasured memories, which to this day I long to re-experience. Now, I find myself forced to traverse these ageless rooms — eerily accurate replicas of the household I had cherished — until I reach a threshold that leads somewhere else. Echoes of my family drum in my ears, as a thousand memories of them flood my mind. I’ve come to believe that nostalgia is a choice. You can choose to feel anguish, missing the better moments of your life, or you can ignore these thoughts that only lead to more homesickness, and be glad that those moments at least happened in a time long gone. For me, this decision is a fighting ground, as I can’t help but root for the first choice. The further I explore this household expanse, the emptier it feels. Not in terms of furnishings, but in their state. Boxes, which I’ll find labeled as “toys” or “dishware”, make me feel as if I were moving out, leaving countless memories behind. Homesickness creeps in as I watch rooms so similar to the ones I had lived in throughout my childhood. It feels as if the level torments me with my past. My mind flashes once more with thoughts of my family’s house. This time, it is different. It is empty, devoid of the familiar items I had gotten so used to seeing. A truck, filled with countless boxes, lays outside. Words I cannot recall are imprinted on its back. I sit in the back of a car, also forgotten in my memory. It speeds off into the distance, away from my home. Home. A house that had been worthy of being called home. It became empty once more, only filled with echoes of countless stories past told.
==Entities== There are no entities here. ==Entrances and Exits== {{Blurimage|file=Liminalhomealtview.webp|width=350px|hovertext=Yellowed Desolation|caption=A different angle of the infamous image location.}} ===⤳ Entrances=== *Falling through a hole on [[Level 0]] and its sub-levels has a low chance of leading one here instead of [[Level 27]]. *Wandering through certain corridors in [[Level 193]] will seamlessly lead here. *Old school stairways in [[Level 153]] can rarely lead one here. *Entering slightly more modern homes in [[Level 271]] will lead here. *Finding a hatch with a staircase on [[Level 269]] and going up it will lead to one of the basements of the level. *It is theorized that [[No-Clipping|noclipping]] out of [[The Frontrooms|reality]] can lead one here under extreme conditions, though it has not been confirmed. ===⤳ Exits === *Noclipping in a yellow room will lead one back to [[Level 0]]. *Additionally, climbing up a hole in the ceiling on the highest floor on '''Level 231''' will also lead back to [[Level 0]]. *Noclipping into a painting with mountain ranges and glass bridges will lead to [[Level 264]]. *Going through a door beside a square window inside a laundromat will lead one to [[Level 232]]. *Attempting to break through the floor will cause one to fall into [[Level 4.11]]. This is not recommended, as one may break parts of the level after falling into it, consequently leading them into the abyss. *Picking up a crayon will begin a proccess that transitions the level into [[Level 233]]. *Watching the entirety of a TV broadcast sends one to [[Level 369]]. ----
